'''Cade Cust''' (born 14 September 1998) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|so}} or {{rlp|hk}} for  [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] in the [[National Rugby League]], [[Wigan Warriors]] and the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Cust was born in [[Scone, New South Wales]], Australia. He is of [[Aboriginal Australians|Aboriginal]] descent.

Cust was a [[Scone Thoroughbreds]] junior.

==Playing career==
===Blacktown Workers Sea Eagles===
At the start of the [[2019 NRL season]], Cust played in the [[Canterbury Cup NSW]] for Manly's feeder club side the [[Blacktown Workers Sea Eagles]].

===Manly Warringah===
In Round 10 2019, Cust made his [[NRL]] debut for Manly Warringah against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] at [[Shark Park]].  Cust scored his first try for Manly in Round 12 2019 against Penrith at [[Penrith Park]] in which Manly lost the match 15-12.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2019-05-30/nrl-round-12-penrith-panthers-beat-manly-sea-eagles/11160454/|title=Penrith Panthers defeat Manly Sea Eagles 15-12 in NRL round 12|website=ABC}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seaeagles.com.au/news/2019/05/19/cade-cust-to-make-nrl-debut/|title=Cade Cust to make NRL debut|website=Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles}}</ref>

Cust was limited to only ten games for Manly in the [[2021 NRL season]].  He did not play in the club's finals campaign which saw Manly reach the preliminary final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-finals-2021-south-sydney-rabbitohs-vs-manly-sea-eagles-tom-trbojevic-live-stream-updates-how-to-watch-news/news-story/27d07d70e5732eed005fa94afec8c9df/|title=GLORY, GLORY: Souths march into the GF as Wayne masterminds Manly mauling|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

===Wigan===
On 12 November 2021, Cust signed a two-year deal with English side [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/59262236|title=Wigan Warriors sign half-back from NRL side Manly Warringah Sea Eagles|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
In round 1 of the 2022 Super League season, Cust made his club debut for Wigan in a 24-10 victory over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/match-report-hull-kr-wigan-warriors-3565355/|title=Wigan Warriors start their Super League campaign with a 24-10 win away to Hull KR|website=www.wigantoday.net}}</ref>
On 28 May 2022, he played for Wigan in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61594770.amp/|title=Challenge Cup final: Huddersfield Giants 14-16 Wigan Warriors|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
In round 24 of the 2022 Super League season, Cust scored two tries in a 52-6 victory over [[Toulouse Olympique]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wigan-52-6-toulouse-bevan-french-scores-hat-trick-as-warriors-strengthen-second-spot/|title=Wigan 52-6 Toulouse: Bevan French scores hat-trick as Warriors strengthen second spot|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>
Cust played 14 games for Wigan in the 2023 Super League season. Cust did not play in Wigan's [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
On 24 October 2023 it was reported that he had signed for [[Salford Red Devils]] for 2024<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL |date=24 Oct 2023|title=Wigan Warriors playmaker Cade Cust to move to Salford Red Devils|url=https://www.totalrl.com/wigan-warriors-playmaker-cade-cust-to-move-to-salford-red-devils/}}</ref>

===Hull F.C.===
On 11 October 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Hull F.C.]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=11 Oct 2024|title=Hull FC bring in Salford half-back Cust|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c89l3yneje4o}}</ref>
In round 1 of the 2025 Super League season, Cust scored two tries on debut for Hull F.C. in their upset victory over Catalans Dragons.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c20p9z9lvg7o|title=Two tries for Cust as new-look Hull FC beat Catalans|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
In round 8 of the 2025 Super League season, Cust was given a straight red card in the first half of Hull F.C.'s match against arch-rivals Hull Kingston Rovers. Hull F.C. would lose the match 28-14.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fcs-cade-cust-learns-10120010/|title=Hull FC's Cade Cust learns disciplinary verdict as fourteen charges handed out|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref>
Cust played 26 games for Hull F.C. in the 2025 Super League season as the club finished 7th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/grading-each-of-super-leagues-bottom-six-in-2025-with-warrington-getting-the-lowest-marks/|title=Grading each of Super League’s bottom six in 2025 with Warrington getting the lowest marks|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>
